old_claim.note: Industrial Standards & Testing Laboratories provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The proving ground was a dedicated weapons-testing facility with proof batteries and ranges for systematic ordnance tests.

source_shape.source_locator: NPS defines a proving ground as where weapons might be tested and describes Sandy Hook's Proof Battery, range, long-range firing, and tests of guns, gunpowder, shells, fuses, and primers.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source describes Sandy Hook as a dedicated industrial weapons-testing site with proof batteries and ranges.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports the broader testing-laboratory prerequisite because the scoped node is an ordnance testing installation rather than a field-use weapon alone.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved and strengthened: industrial_ballistics_laboratories keeps industrial_laboratories_standard_testing as a direct dependency, now source-backed.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to a single weapon design rather than a test facility.
A stronger source showed Sandy Hook did not conduct systematic ordnance testing.
The industrial_laboratories_standard_testing node were narrowed to exclude proving grounds, ranges, or performance-test facilities.
short_reason: Sandy Hook's source-backed scope is a dedicated test facility.
